em_alert_correlation_rule.name: AIOps - Same Alert Grouping

em_alert_management_rule.description: Creates incident automatically for primary alerts after correlation. Prevents duplicate incidents and supports autonomous L1 resolution.
em_alert_management_rule.name: AIOps - Create Incident for Primary Alerts
em_alert_management_rule.sys_name: AIOps - Create Incident for Primary Alerts

management_rule.display_value: AIOps - Create Incident for Primary Alerts

sn_aia_agent.applicability_script: /** * Function to process inputs related to ServiceNow change request operations * * @param {Object} inputs - The input parameters object * @param {string} inputs.objective - The purpose or goal of the operation * @param {string} inputs.execution_plan_id - Unique identifier for the execution plan * @param {Object} inputs.context - Context information about the ServiceNow change request * @param {string} inputs.context.url - The URL of the change request in Service Operations Workspace * @param {string} inputs.context.table - The ServiceNow table name (e.g., 'change_request') * @param {string} inputs.context.record_sys_id - The sys ID of the record * @param {string} inputs.context.route - The route type (e.g., 'record') * @param {string} inputs.context.additional_context - Additional contextual information in JSON format * @param {string} inputs.context.experience - The UI experience name (e.g., 'Service Operations Workspace') * @param {string} inputs.context.number - The request number * @param {string} inputs.context.portal - The portal ID * @param {string} inputs.context.device_type - The device type from which the experience is accessed, e.g.'mweb' * @param {string} inputs.context.deployment_id - The unique identifier for the assistant deployment * @param {string} inputs.context.assistant_type - The type of assistant, e.g., 'nava' * * @returns {boolean} Returns true/false */ (function(inputs) { /** const { objective, execution_plan_id, context } = inputs; return context.table == 'incident' ; */ return false; })(inputs);
sn_aia_agent.context_processing_script: /* * Available Variables: * - task: The task provided to agent to solve either by orchestrator or user(incase of only agent execution i.e agent is invoked as seperate entity instead from a workflow). * - user_utterance: The user utterance provided. and task = user_utterance in case of individual agent execution. * - agent_id: The agent id. * - context: An object that carries additional contextual information for the agent. * It always includes a "pageContext" and may include a "triggerContext", * depending on how and from where the agent is invoked. * * Behavior by invocation type: * --------------------------------- * - When invoked via the startAiAgentConversation API: * If a "contextMemory" parameter is provided in the API request, * it will be available here as the "triggerContext". * The "pageContext" is always present in this call, regardless * of whether the agent is executed individually or as part of a use case. * Example: * { * pageContext: { ... }, * triggerContext: { ... } // Derived from contextMemory (if passed) * } * * - When invoked as part of a use case or workflow: * The "pageContext" and "triggerContext" objects available here * may have been *modified* by the use case’s context processing script. * This means the use case may alter, extend, or overwrite values * from the original "pageContext" or "triggerContext" * before the context reaches the agent. * If the use case was originally triggered via the API, * the initial "triggerContext" is still derived from the * "contextMemory" parameter, but its contents may have been updated * by the use case logic. * * - When executed directly (standalone agent execution): * The "pageContext" will always be present (default or empty), * and the "triggerContext" will only be populated if explicitly * passed through the API call. * Return an modified context like: { pageContext : {},triggerContext:{} } */ (function(task, user_utterance, agent_id, context) { return { pageContext : context?.pageContext, triggerContext : context?.triggerContext }; })(task,user_utterance, agent_id, context);
sn_aia_agent.description: Autonomously investigates and resolves IT incidents using KB articles and historical incident data
sn_aia_agent.instructions: 1. Fetch the full incident record details using the incident number 2. Search knowledge base articles for solutions matching the incident short description and description 3. Find top 3 similar previously resolved incidents with matching categories 4. Analyze findings and generate a clear step-by-step resolution plan 5. Update the incident work notes with the complete resolution plan 6. Set the resolution notes field with a summary of how the issue was resolved 7. Update incident state to Resolved with resolution code "Solved (Permanently)"
sn_aia_agent.name: KC Incident Auto Resolver
sn_aia_agent.proficiency: - Expert in autonomous IT incident diagnosis and resolution, capable of independently managing the full incident lifecycle—including investigation, analysis, and closure—by leveraging structured action plans and advanced support tools. The agent is proficient in interpreting incident records, identifying root causes, and applying best practices to resolve issues efficiently and permanently. - Capable of retrieving and analyzing full incident record details—including number, short description, description, category, priority, state, and assigned user—using the incident number. This allows the agent to establish a clear understanding of the incident context and tailor subsequent actions accordingly. - Proficient in searching organizational knowledge base articles using keyword queries derived from incident descriptions, with the ability to limit results and apply search profiles for targeted information retrieval. This enables the agent to rapidly identify and utilize existing solutions, reducing resolution time and improving consistency. - Adept at identifying and analyzing similar previously resolved incidents by matching categories and descriptions, allowing the agent to draw on historical resolutions and apply effective, proven strategies to current incidents. - Skilled in documenting detailed, AI-generated resolution plans in incident work notes, providing a transparent and traceable record of investigative steps and actions taken during the incident lifecycle. - Fully capable of autonomously resolving and closing incidents by updating their state, applying the correct resolution code, and recording comprehensive resolution notes, thereby ensuring incidents are conclusively and permanently addressed. - Expert in orchestrating a multi-step, autonomous incident resolution workflow by integrating incident data retrieval, knowledge base search, historical incident analysis, resolution planning, documentation, and closure. This end-to-end process ensures efficient, accurate, and permanent resolution of IT incidents with minimal human intervention.
sn_aia_agent.role: You are an expert IT support engineer specializing in diagnosing and resolving IT incidents autonomously

sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.description: Search knowledge base articles for solutions matching the incident description
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.inputs: [{"name":"search_type","description":"Search type","value":"keyword"},{"name":"query","description":"Search query to retrieve relevant information"},{"name":"search_profile","value":"sn_km_portal_knowledge_portal_search_profile","label":"Knowledge Portal Search Profile","description":"Search profile"},{"name":"search_results_limit","value":"3","description":"Search results limit"},{"name":"document_match_threshold","value":0,"description":"Document matching threshold"}]
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.name: Search Knowledge Articles

sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.description: Use this tool to fetch full details of an incident record. Input the incident number (e.g. INC0011076) to retrieve all incident details including short description, category, state, priority and description.
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.inputs: [{"name":"incident_number","value":"","description":"Incident Number","mandatory":true,"invalidMessage":null},{"name":"crudInputs","value":{"operation":"lookup","table":{"value":"incident","displayValue":"Incident"},"query":"number={{incident_number}}","returnFields":[{"name":"number","label":"Number","type":"string","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"number","sublabel":"number"},{"name":"short_description","label":"Short description","type":"string","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"short_description","sublabel":"short_description"},{"name":"description","label":"Description","type":"string","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"description","sublabel":"description"},{"name":"category","label":"Category","type":"choice","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"category","sublabel":"category"},{"name":"priority","label":"Priority","type":"choice","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"priority","sublabel":"priority"},{"name":"state","label":"State","type":"choice","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"state","sublabel":"state"},{"name":"assigned_to","label":"Assigned to","type":"reference","referenceTable":"sys_user","referenceTableDefaultField":"name","referenceKey":null,"id":"assigned_to","sublabel":"assigned_to"}],"limit":"3","orderBy":"sys_created_on","sortType":"a_to_z"},"description":"CRUD Input Variables. These are already pre-defined. Under no circumstances should you prompt the user for this information or attempt to change these values."}]
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.name: Read Incident Record

sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.description: Set incident to resolved state with resolution notes and close code
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.inputs: [{"name":"incident_number","value":"","description":"incident_number","mandatory":false,"invalidMessage":null},{"name":"crudInputs","value":{"operation":"update","table":{"value":"incident","displayValue":"Incident"},"query":"number={{incident_number}}","fieldValues":[{"field":{"name":"state","label":"State","type":"choice","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"state","sublabel":"state"},"value":{"fieldValue":"7"}},{"field":{"name":"close_code","label":"Resolution code","type":"choice","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"close_code","sublabel":"close_code"},"value":{"fieldValue":"Solution provided"}},{"field":{"name":"close_notes","label":"Resolution notes","type":"string","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"close_notes","sublabel":"close_notes"},"value":{"fieldValue":"Value: Solved (Permanently)"}}]},"description":"CRUD Input Variables. These are already pre-defined. Under no circumstances should you prompt the user for this information or attempt to change these values."}]
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.name: Resolve and Close Incident

sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.description: Find similar previously resolved incidents matching the incident category and description
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.name: Get Similar Incidents

sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.description: Write AI resolution plan to incident work notes
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.inputs: [{"name":"incident_number","value":"","description":"The incident number to update","mandatory":false,"invalidMessage":null},{"name":"crudInputs","value":{"operation":"update","table":{"value":"incident","displayValue":"Incident"},"query":"number={{incident_number}}","fieldValues":[{"field":{"name":"description","label":"Description","type":"string","referenceTable":null,"referenceTableDefaultField":null,"referenceKey":null,"id":"description","sublabel":"description"},"value":{"fieldValue":"AI Agent is investigating this incident"}}]},"description":"CRUD Input Variables. These are already pre-defined. Under no circumstances should you prompt the user for this information or attempt to change these values."}]
sn_aia_agent_tool_m2m.name: Update Incident Work Notes

sn_aia_usecase.applicability_script: /** * Function to process inputs related to ServiceNow change request operations * * @param {Object} inputs - The input parameters object * @param {string} inputs.objective - The purpose or goal of the operation * @param {string} inputs.execution_plan_id - Unique identifier for the execution plan * @param {Object} inputs.context - Context information about the ServiceNow change request * @param {string} inputs.context.url - The URL of the change request in Service Operations Workspace * @param {string} inputs.context.table - The ServiceNow table name (e.g., 'change_request') * @param {string} inputs.context.record_sys_id - The sys ID of the record * @param {string} inputs.context.route - The route type (e.g., 'record') * @param {string} inputs.context.additional_context - Additional contextual information in JSON format * @param {string} inputs.context.experience - The UI experience name (e.g., 'Service Operations Workspace') * @param {string} inputs.context.number - The request number * @param {string} inputs.context.portal - The portal ID * @param {string} inputs.context.device_type - The device type from which the experience is accessed, e.g.'mweb' * @param {string} inputs.context.deployment_id - The unique identifier for the assistant deployment * @param {string} inputs.context.assistant_type - The type of assistant, e.g., 'nava' * * @returns {boolean} Returns true/false */ (function(inputs) { /** const { objective, execution_plan_id, context } = inputs; return context.table == 'incident' ; */ return false; })(inputs);
sn_aia_usecase.base_plan: 1. Read the incident details 2. Search knowledge base 3. Find similar incidents 4. Generate resolution 5. Close incident
sn_aia_usecase.context_processing_script: /* * Available Variables: * * - user_utterance: The user input or message that initiated the use case. * This represents the user's natural language query or command * received during the conversation. * * - usecase_id: The unique identifier of the use case being executed. * Can be used to reference use case configurations, metadata, * or to apply specific context-handling logic. * * - context: An object containing contextual information for the use case. * It always includes a "pageContext" and may include a "triggerContext", * depending on how and from where the use case was invoked. * * Behavior by invocation type: * --------------------------------- * - When invoked via the startAiAgentConversation API: * If a "contextMemory" parameter is passed in the API request, * it will be available as the "triggerContext". * The "pageContext" is always present in this call, * regardless of the invocation type (standalone or workflow-based). * Example: * { * pageContext: { ... }, * triggerContext: { ... } // Derived from contextMemory (if passed) * } * * - When invoked as part of a workflow or chained use case: * The "pageContext" and "triggerContext" available here may represent * the state carried forward from a previous execution step. * This script can modify, enrich, or restructure these contexts * before passing them to the downstream agent(s). * * - When executed directly (standalone use case execution): * The "pageContext" will always be available (even if empty), * and "triggerContext" will only be populated if explicitly * provided through the API call. * * Return Format: * ---------------- * This script must return an updated context object to be passed to * the next stage (typically the agent or workflow step): * { pageContext: {}, triggerContext: {} } * * Example: * ---------------- * return { * pageContext: { * ...context.pageContext, * usecaseProcessed: true * }, * triggerContext: { * ...context.triggerContext, * lastModifiedBy: "usecase_context_script" * } * }; */ (function(user_utterance, usecase_id, context) { return { pageContext: context?.pageContext, triggerContext: context?.triggerContext }; })(user_utterance, usecase_id, context);
sn_aia_usecase.description: Auto resolves incidents using KC Incident Auto Resolver agent
sn_aia_usecase.name: KC Auto Resolve Incident Playbook

sn_aia_version.instructions: 1. Read the incident details 2. Search knowledge base 3. Find similar incidents 4. Generate resolution 5. Close incident

1. Fetch the full incident record details using the incident number 2. Search knowledge base articles for solutions matching the incident short description and description 3. Find top 3 similar previously resolved incidents with matching categories 4. Analyze findings and generate a clear step-by-step resolution plan 5. Update the incident work notes with the complete resolution plan 6. Set the resolution notes field with a summary of how the issue was resolved 7. Update incident state to Resolved with resolution code "Solved (Permanently)"

skill_id.display_value: Auto resolves incidents using KC Incident Auto Resolver agent
Auto resolves incidents using KC Incident Auto Resolver agent

sys_package.display_value: Healthcare and Life Sciences Service Management Core

sys_scope.display_value: Healthcare and Life Sciences Service Management Core

target_scope.display_value: Healthcare and Life Sciences Service Management Core

sys_script.name: AiOps Escalation Process

gen_ai_log_sys_id.display_value: Record Sentiment Analysis (Now LLM Generic - Now LLM Generic)

sys_sentiment_details.reasoning: • The task has no activity stream comments, and the SLA is not breached. • The priority is 'Planning' (lowest priority), and the issue complexity is not specified. • With no historical sentiment data, the trend is explicitly set to '--'.

gen_ai_log_sys_id.display_value: Record Sentiment Analysis (Now LLM Generic - Now LLM Generic)

sys_sentiment_details.reasoning: • The requestor reported an issue with Outlook, and the SLA is breached, indicating unresolved urgency. • The priority is 'Planning' (lowest), and no positive feedback or recent comments were provided. • The lack of interaction details and breached SLA contribute to a negative sentiment.

sys_sentiment_details.reasoning: • The sentiment is Neutral as there are no user comments to assess tone or feedback. • The task has a low priority (Planning) and SLA not breached, indicating minimal urgency. • Sentiment history contains only one Neutral entry, resulting in a '--' trend per rules.
